title: Crispy Middle-eastern Enab Warak Enab
description: A traditional Middle-eastern dish featuring fermented and crispy flatbread filled with spiced ingredients. This recipe is easy to make and can be enjoyed as a snack or dessert.

ingredients.name: Lebanese cheese (or substitute feta or ricotta)

instructions: Combine yeast, flour, turmeric powder, and salt in a mixing bowl to make the dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes and let it rest for 1 hour.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch and cut into 6-8 pieces.
Fry the dough pieces in hot ghee or oil until crispy and golden brown.
Drain the fried dough on paper towels and let it cool slightly.
Mix the minced onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, and dried mint in a bowl.
Add the yogurt and mix well to create a crumbly mixture.
Place a spoonful of the yogurt mixture on each fried dough piece and top with a spoonful of Lebanese cheese.
Serve the crispy flatbread warm and enjoy!
